instruction: Deploy WordPress on my OneKE cluster with a dedicated namespace and persistent storage
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy WordPress on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - wordpress
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create wordpress namespace"
    params:
      name: wordpress
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for WordPress data"
    params:
      name: wordpress-data
      namespace: wordpress
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy WordPress via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
      release_name: wordpress
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: wordpress-data
        service:
          type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for WordPress pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: wordpress
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=wordpress"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the WordPress access URL"
    params:
      service_name: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify WordPress pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove WordPress Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: wordpress
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the WordPress PVC"
      params:
        name: wordpress-data
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the wordpress namespace"
      params:
        name: wordpress

instruction: Remove the Redis deployment from the production namespace on my OneKE cluster
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Remove Redis deployment from production namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "3 minutes"
  risk_level: high
  tags:
    - redis
    - cleanup
    - production
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Check current Redis deployment status before removal"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.uninstall
    description: "Uninstall Redis Helm release"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    on_failure: abort
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify production namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Redis pods are terminated"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.deploy
      description: "Re-deploy Redis if removal was unintended"
      params:
        chart: redis
        namespace: production
        release_name: redis
        repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

instruction: Scale my OneKE cluster to 5 worker nodes
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Scale OneKE cluster to 5 worker nodes"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - scaling
    - cluster
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.cluster.get_status
    description: "Check current cluster status before scaling"
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.cluster.list_nodes
    description: "List current nodes to verify baseline"
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
    description: "Scale cluster to 5 worker nodes"
    params:
      worker_count: 5
    depends_on:
      - step_02
    timeout_seconds: 600
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is healthy and reachable"
  post_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is still healthy after scaling"

instruction: Deploy my application on AWS EKS using Fargate serverless containers
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy application on AWS EKS using Fargate"
  risk_level: high
steps: []
error:
  is_error: true
  reason: "AWS EKS and Fargate are not supported by this tool. This system manages OpenNebula infrastructure and OneKE Kubernetes clusters only."
  suggestion: "Use the AWS CLI or eksctl to manage EKS clusters. If you want to deploy on your OneKE cluster instead, rephrase your request to target OneKE."

instruction: Create a namespace called monitoring, deploy Prometheus with a 50Gi volume, and expose its web UI
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up Prometheus monitoring with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "8 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - prometheus
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create monitoring namespace"
    params:
      name: monitoring
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Prometheus data"
    params:
      name: prometheus-data
      namespace: monitoring
      size: 50Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Prometheus via Helm"
    params:
      chart: prometheus
      namespace: monitoring
      release_name: prometheus
      repo_url: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        server:
          persistentVolume:
            existingClaim: prometheus-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Prometheus to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: monitoring
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Prometheus web UI via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: prometheus-server
      namespace: monitoring
      port: 9090
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Prometheus is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Prometheus service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Prometheus"
      params:
        release_name: prometheus
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Prometheus storage"
      params:
        name: prometheus-data
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove monitoring namespace"
      params:
        name: monitoring

instruction: Create a new virtual machine with 4 CPUs and 8GB of RAM using template ID 5
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a virtual machine with 4 CPUs and 8GB RAM from template 5"
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: low
  tags:
    - vm
    - opennebula
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.create
    description: "Create VM from template 5"
    params:
      template_id: 5
      name: webserver-01
      cpu: 4
      memory_mb: 8192
    timeout_seconds: 300
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: resource_available
      description: "Verify sufficient resources for VM creation"
  post_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Verify VM was created successfully"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: one.vm.delete
      description: "Delete VM if creation failed"
      params:
        vm_id: 0

instruction: Power off virtual machine 42 and take a snapshot before the maintenance window
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Power off VM 42 and create a pre-maintenance snapshot"
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - vm
    - maintenance
    - snapshot
    - opennebula
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.poweroff
    description: "Power off the VM gracefully"
    params:
      vm_id: 42
    timeout_seconds: 120
  - id: step_02
    action: one.vm.snapshot_create
    description: "Create a pre-maintenance snapshot"
    params:
      vm_id: 42
      snapshot_name: pre-maintenance-snapshot
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    timeout_seconds: 300
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Verify VM 42 exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Verify snapshot was created"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: one.vm.resume
      description: "Resume the VM if snapshot failed"
      params:
        vm_id: 42

instruction: List all virtual machines and all applications running on the OneKE cluster
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "List all VMs and OneKE cluster applications"
  estimated_duration: "1 minute"
  risk_level: low
  tags:
    - inventory
    - vm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.list
    description: "List all OpenNebula virtual machines"
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.list
    description: "List all Helm releases on the OneKE cluster"
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.cluster.get_info
    description: "Get OneKE cluster information"

instruction: Deploy Nginx using Helm on the default namespace
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Nginx on the default namespace using Helm"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: low
  tags:
    - nginx
    - helm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Nginx via Helm chart to default namespace"
    params:
      chart: nginx
      namespace: default
      release_name: nginx
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      create_namespace: false
    timeout_seconds: 300
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Nginx pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: default
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=nginx"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the Nginx service endpoint"
    params:
      service_name: nginx
      namespace: default
    depends_on:
      - step_02
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify default namespace is accessible"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Nginx pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Nginx Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: nginx
        namespace: default

instruction: Install Redis in the production namespace on my OneKE cluster
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Install Redis in existing production namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - redis
    - production
    - helm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Redis via Helm to production namespace"
    params:
      chart: redis
      namespace: production
      release_name: redis
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      create_namespace: false
      values:
        architecture: standalone
        auth:
          enabled: true
    timeout_seconds: 300
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Redis pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: production
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=redis"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_01
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify production namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Redis pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Redis Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: redis
        namespace: production

instruction: Deploy Grafana into the existing monitoring namespace and expose it on port 3000
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Grafana into existing monitoring namespace with external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: low
  tags:
    - grafana
    - monitoring
    - helm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana via Helm to monitoring namespace"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: monitoring
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
      create_namespace: false
      values:
        persistence:
          enabled: true
          size: 5Gi
    timeout_seconds: 300
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: monitoring
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Grafana dashboard via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: grafana
      namespace: monitoring
      port: 3000
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_02
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify monitoring namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Grafana is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Grafana service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: monitoring
